is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study determines genetic diversity of Plasmodium falciparum infections in 107 travelers and analyzes dhfr mutations.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfdhfr codons 51, 59, 108 (and 164): 'always the Asn-108 mutation and often the Ile-51 and Arg-59 mutations' with counts in Tables 2 and 3.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 3 gives wt/mt counts per codon (108: 0 Ser/21 Asn among proguanil-yes; 51: 3 wt/18 mt; etc.).

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples are from travelers returning to Sweden who visited 38 countries; mutation frequencies are pooled across travelers, not tied to any sub-national location within an African country. Data grouped only by broad region (East/West Africa, Asia, South America) and by drug intake.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Patients admitted in Stockholm between 1995 and 1998 — approximately a 4-year window, but frequencies not resolvable to specific sub-3-year bins; span slightly exceeds 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Samples are from travelers/imported cases diagnosed in Sweden, not field/clinical isolates collected in an African country. Explicitly a traveler study.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study genotyping 107 patient samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Routine collection of malaria patients admitted at two Stockholm hospitals 1995-1998; no consortium/network overlap stated.

supplement.evidence: All mutation frequency data presented in Tables 2 and 3 within the main text; no supplementary pointer.

countries: Sweden (travelers from The Gambia, Ghana, Kenya, and other African/Asian/South American countries)
assessment.summary: Traveler study in Sweden (1995-1998) genotyping P. falciparum dhfr mutations (codons 51, 59, 108, 164) in returning travelers. Reports extractable per-codon wt/mt frequencies, but samples are imported traveler cases (not African field isolates) and cannot be tied to sub-national locations within any African country, since frequencies are pooled across travelers who visited 38 countries.
exclusion_reasons: Samples are imported/traveller cases diagnosed in Sweden, not field/clinical isolates collected in an African country
No sub-country geographic resolution: mutation frequencies are pooled across travelers and cannot be tied to sub-national locations
Collection span (1995-1998) exceeds 3 years for pooled frequencies
